head.hi: The Brazilian Embassy to the Department of State

div.p: The Brazilian Government would be very grateful if the Government of the United States of America would be good enough to designate two of its Army officers, specialists in coast artillery, to serve as instructors in the Brazilian Army Center of Instruction. One of these officers should possess special knowledge in the subject of fire tactics and the other in the subject of tactical organization. With regard to the respective remuneration, the Brazilian Government suggests that the terms for the American officers contracted for the service of the Brazilian NavyContract signed June 25, 1932, not printed. It provided for a small naval mission composed of two officers and one chief petty officer of the United States Navy to assist in the work of instruction at the Brazilian War College (Department of State, Press Releases, June 25, 1932, p. 597). serve as a basis for the same.
